action.reasoning:
Observation: Best is iter-4 with MSE 0.4867. Iter-10 reproduction gave 0.5299, despite same code. Differences: iter-4 used 8 epochs at lr=5e-3, but cosine LR over 8 vs 15 epochs differs. Iter-10 cosine over 15 epochs picked best at epoch 1 (val 1.27). Actually the best val 1.27 at epoch 1 corresponds to test 0.53, but iter-4 saved val 1.22 -> test 0.487. Different training dynamics.

Analysis: Need to try a stronger approach. NLinear-shared (one shared linear with last-value subtraction) is known to be best on ETTh1 96->720, with MSE around 0.43-0.46. Let me try NLinear (subtract last value, one linear seq->pred per channel, like DLinear without decomp).

Decision: Build NLinear (subtract last val, channel-independent linear, no decomp) and also keep best ckpt from iter-4 by NOT overwriting if val doesn't improve. Actually I should try NLinear and DLinear-without-decomp variants. Use longer training with very low LR.
